input.prompt:
I need to understand how compound components are built in a shadcn/ui component library project. Please explore:

1. Find components that use React context internally to share state between sub-components — good examples would be Accordion, Tabs, or similar compound components
2. For 2-3 such components, read the full component source code so I can understand:
   - How they define and use React context
   - How they export multiple named sub-components
   - How they handle orientation (horizontal vs vertical)
   - What TypeScript interfaces/types they define
   - How they use Radix UI primitives (if at all)
   - How they use cn() utility, cva() variants, etc.
3. Also look for any Stepper-like component that might already exist

Focus on components in the `components/ui/` directory or similar. Read the actual file contents.

14	const SHADCN_VERSION = "latest"

16	export { BASES, type Base }
17	export { STYLES, type Style }
18	export { THEMES, type Theme }

23	export type BaseName = Base["name"]
24	export type StyleName = Style["name"]
25	export type ThemeName = Theme["name"]
26	export type BaseColorName = BaseColor["name"]
27	export type ChartColorName = Theme["name"]
28	
29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const
35	
36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}
54

56	  { value: "subtle", label: "Subtle" },

60	export type MenuAccent = (typeof MENU_ACCENTS)[number]
61	export type MenuAccentValue = MenuAccent["value"]

74	export const RADII = [
75	  { name: "default", label: "Default", value: "" },
76	  { name: "none", label: "None", value: "0" },
77	  { name: "small", label: "Small", value: "0.45rem" },
78	  { name: "medium", label: "Medium", value: "0.625rem" },
79	  { name: "large", label: "Large", value: "0.875rem" },
80	] as const
81	
82	export type Radius = (typeof RADII)[number]
83	
84	export type RadiusValue = Radius["name"]
85	
86	export const designSystemConfigSchema = z
87	  .object({
88	    base: z.enum(BASES.map((b) => b.name) as [BaseName, ...BaseName[]]),
89	    style: z.enum(STYLES.map((s) => s.name) as [StyleName, ...StyleName[]]),
90	    iconLibrary: z.enum(
91	      Object.keys(iconLibraries) as [IconLibraryName, ...IconLibraryName[]]
92	    ),
93	    baseColor: z
94	      .enum(
95	        BASE_COLORS.map((c) => c.name) as [BaseColorName, ...BaseColorName[]]
96	      )
97	      .default("neutral"),
98	    theme: z.enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ThemeName, ...ThemeName[]]),
99	    chartColor: z
100	      .enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ChartColorName, ...ChartColorName[]])
101	      .default("neutral"),
102	    font: z.enum(fontValues).default("inter"),
103	    fontHeading: z.enum(fontHeadingValues).default("inherit"),
104	    item: z.string().optional(),
105	    rtl: z.boolean().default(false),
106	    menuAccent: z
107	      .enum(
108	        MENU_ACCENTS.map((a) => a.value) as [
109	          MenuAccentValue,
110	          ...MenuAccentValue[],
111	        ]
112	      )
113	      .default("subtle"),
114	    menuColor: z
115	      .enum(
116	        MENU_COLORS.map((m) => m.value) as [MenuColorValue, ...MenuColorValue[]]
117	      )
118	      .default("default"),
119	    radius: z
120	      .enum(RADII.map((r) => r.name) as [RadiusValue, ...RadiusValue[]])
121	      .default("default"),
122	    template: z
123	      .enum([
124	        "next",
125	        "next-monorepo",
126	        "start",
127	        "react-router",
128	        "vite",
129	        "vite-monorepo",
130	        "react-router-monorepo",
131	        "start-monorepo",
132	        "astro",
133	        "astro-monorepo",
134	        "laravel",
135	      ])
136	      .default("next")
137	      .optional(),
138	  })
139	  .refine(
140	    (data) => {
141	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
142	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.theme)
143	    },
144	    (data) => ({
145	      message: `Theme "${data.theme}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
146	      path: ["theme"],
147	    })
148	  )
149	  .refine(
150	    (data) => {
151	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
152	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.chartColor)
153	    },
154	    (data) => ({
155	      message: `Chart color "${data.chartColor}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
156	      path: ["chartColor"],
157	    })
158	  )

407	export function getThemesForBaseColor(baseColorName: string) {
408	  const baseColorNames = BASE_COLORS.map((bc) => bc.name)
409	
410	  return THEMES.filter((theme) => {
411	    if (theme.name === baseColorName) {
412	      return true
413	    }
414	    return !baseColorNames.includes(theme.name)
415	  })
416	}
417	
418	export function getBase(name: BaseName) {
419	  return BASES.find((base) => base.name === name)
420	}
421	
422	export function getStyle(name: StyleName) {
423	  return STYLES.find((style) => style.name === name)
424	}
425	
426	export function getTheme(name: ThemeName) {
427	  return THEMES.find((theme) => theme.name === name)
428	}
429	
430	export function getBaseColor(name: BaseColorName) {
431	  return BASE_COLORS.find((color) => color.name === name)
432	}
433	
434	export function getIconLibrary(name: IconLibraryName) {
435	  return iconLibraries[name]
436	}
437	
438	// Builds a registry:theme item from a design system config.
439	export function buildRegistryTheme(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
440	  const baseColor = getBaseColor(config.baseColor)
441	  const theme = getTheme(config.theme)
442	
443	  if (!baseColor || !theme) {
444	    throw new Error(
445	      `Base color "${config.baseColor}" or theme "${config.theme}" not found`
446	    )
447	  }
448	
449	  // Merge base color and theme CSS vars.
450	  const lightVars: Record<string, string> = {
451	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
452	    ...(theme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
453	  }
454	  const darkVars: Record<string, string> = {
455	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
456	    ...(theme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
457	  }
458	  const themeVars: Record<string, string> = {}
459	
460	  // Apply chart color override.
461	  const chartTheme = getTheme(config.chartColor)
462	  if (chartTheme) {
463	    const chartLight = chartTheme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>
464	    const chartDark = chartTheme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>
465	    for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
466	      const key = `chart-${i}`
467	      if (chartLight?.[key]) lightVars[key] = chartLight[key]
468	      if (chartDark?.[key]) darkVars[key] = chartDark[key]
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  // Apply menu accent transformation.
473	  if (config.menuAccent === "bold") {
474	    lightVars.accent = lightVars.primary
475	    lightVars["accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
476	    darkVars.accent = darkVars.primary
477	    darkVars["accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
478	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent"] = lightVars.primary
479	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
480	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent"] = darkVars.primary
481	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
482	  }
483	
484	  // Apply radius transformation.
485	  if (config.radius && config.radius !== "default") {
486	    const radius = RADII.find((r) => r.name === config.radius)
487	    if (radius && radius.value) {
488	      lightVars.radius = radius.value
489	    }
490	  }
491	
492	  return {
493	    name: `${config.baseColor}-${config.theme}`,
494	    type: "registry:theme" as const,
495	    cssVars: {
496	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
497	      light: lightVars,
498	      dark: darkVars,
499	    },
500	  }
501	}
502	
503	// Builds a registry:base item from a design system config.
504	export function buildRegistryBase(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
505	  const baseItem = getBase(config.base)
506	  const iconLibraryItem = getIconLibrary(config.iconLibrary)
507	  const normalizedFontHeading =
508	    config.fontHeading === config.font ? "inherit" : config.fontHeading
509	
510	  if (!baseItem || !iconLibraryItem) {
511	    throw new Error(
512	      `Base "${config.base}" or icon library "${config.iconLibrary}" not found`
513	    )
514	  }
515	
516	  const registryTheme = buildRegistryTheme(config)
517	
518	  // Build dependencies.
519	  const dependencies = [
520	    `shadcn@${SHADCN_VERSION}`,
521	    "class-variance-authority",
522	    "tw-animate-css",
523	    ...(baseItem.dependencies ?? []),
524	    ...iconLibraryItem.packages,
525	  ]
526	
527	  const registryDependencies = ["utils"]
528	  const themeVars = {
529	    ...(registryTheme.cssVars?.theme ?? {}),
530	    ...(normalizedFontHeading === "inherit"
531	      ? { "--font-heading": getInheritedHeadingFontValue(config.font) }
532	      : {}),
533	  }
534	
535	  if (config.font) {
536	    registryDependencies.push(`font-${config.font}`)
537	  }
538	
539	  if (normalizedFontHeading !== "inherit") {
540	    registryDependencies.push(`font-heading-${normalizedFontHeading}`)
541	  }
542	
543	  return {
544	    name: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
545	    extends: "none",
546	    type: "registry:base" as const,
547	    config: {
548	      style: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
549	      iconLibrary: iconLibraryItem.name,
550	      rtl: config.rtl,
551	      menuColor: config.menuColor,
552	      menuAccent: config.menuAccent,
553	      tailwind: {
554	        baseColor: config.baseColor,
555	      },
556	    },
557	    dependencies,
558	    registryDependencies,
559	    cssVars: {
560	      ...registryTheme.cssVars,
561	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
562	    },
563	    css: {
564	      '@import "tw-animate-css"': {},
565	      '@import "shadcn/tailwind.css"': {},
566	      "@layer base": {
567	        "*": { "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {} },
568	        body: { "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {} },
569	      },
570	    },
571	    ...(config.rtl && {
572	      docs: `To learn how to set up the RTL provider and fonts for your app, see https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/rtl/${config.template === "next-monorepo" ? "next" : (config.template ?? "next")}`,
573	    }),
574	  }
575	}

24	/*
25	 * build-registry.mts is the single v4 registry pipeline.
26	 *
27	 * Source of truth:
28	 * - Authored raw component/registry source lives in registry/bases/base and
29	 *   registry/bases/radix.
30	 * - Authored demo source lives in examples/base and examples/radix.
31	 * - Style tokens live in registry/styles/style-*.css.
32	 *
33	 * Persistent outputs:
34	 * - registry/bases/__index__.tsx
35	 * - registry/__index__.tsx
36	 * - examples/__index__.tsx
37	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui/*
38	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
39	 * - public/r/*
40	 *
41	 * Temporary outputs:
42	 * - registry/<base-style>/*
43	 * - registry-<style>.json
44	 *
45	 * Execution order:
46	 * 1. Build registry/bases/__index__.tsx from the authored base registries.
47	 * 2. Build temporary styled registries under registry/<base-style>.
48	 * 3. Build registry/__index__.tsx for runtime lookup across legacy styles and
49	 *    generated base-style combinations.
50	 * 4. Build examples/__index__.tsx from authored demos.
51	 * 5. Export public/r/* for every style through the shadcn CLI.
52	 * 6. Copy compiled ui/* from the temporary registries into styles/<style>/ui.
53	 * 7. Build styles/<style>/ui-rtl for base-nova and radix-nova only.
54	 * 8. Format the generated persistent outputs.
55	 * 9. Clean up the temporary registry/<base-style> trees and registry-*.json.
56	 */

145	function hashContent(...parts: string[]) {
146	  const hash = createHash("sha256")
147	
148	  for (const part of parts) {
149	    hash.update(part)
150	    hash.update("\0")
151	  }
152	
153	  return hash.digest("hex")
154	}

156	async function readFileIfExists(filePath: string) {
157	  try {
158	    return await fs.readFile(filePath, "utf8")
159	  } catch (error) {
160	    if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === "ENOENT") {
161	      return null
162	    }
163	
164	    throw error
165	  }
166	}
167	
168	async function writeIfChanged(filePath: string, content: string) {
169	  const existingContent = await readFileIfExists(filePath)
170	  if (existingContent === content) {
171	    return false
172	  }
173	
174	  await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true })
175	  await fs.writeFile(filePath, content)
176	
177	  return true
178	}
179	
180	async function formatGeneratedSource(content: string, filePath: string) {
181	  prettierConfigPromise ??= prettier.resolveConfig(
182	    path.join(process.cwd(), "package.json")
183	  )
184	
185	  const prettierConfig = (await prettierConfigPromise) ?? {}
186	
187	  return prettier.format(content, {
188	    ...prettierConfig,
189	    filepath: filePath,
190	  })
191	}
192	
193	async function formatGeneratedJson(value: unknown, filePath: string) {
194	  return formatGeneratedSource(JSON.stringify(value, null, 2), filePath)
195	}

277	  z.string()

280	// New schema for getRegistries().

282	  z.object({
283	    name: z.string(),
284	    homepage: z.string().optional(),
285	    url: z.string(),
286	    description: z.string().optional(),
287	  })
288	)
289	
290	export const presetSchema = z.object({
291	  name: z.string(),
292	  title: z.string(),
293	  description: z.string(),
294	  base: z.string(),
295	  style: z.string(),
296	  baseColor: z.string(),
297	  theme: z.string(),
298	  iconLibrary: z.string(),
299	  font: z.string(),
300	  rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
301	  menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]),
302	  menuColor: z.enum([
303	    "default",
304	    "inverted",
305	    "default-translucent",
306	    "inverted-translucent",
307	  ]),
308	  radius: z.string(),
309	})
310	
311	export type Preset = z.infer<typeof presetSchema>
312	
313	export const configJsonSchema = z.object({
314	  presets: z.array(presetSchema),
315	})
316	
317	export type ConfigJson = z.infer<typeof configJsonSchema>

Excellent! Now let me compile a comprehensive summary of my findings. Let me create a final report:

## Comprehensive Report: Compound Components in shadcn/ui

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library and analyzed how compound components are structured. Here are my key findings:

### **1. Core Patterns for Compound Components**

The shadcn/ui library demonstrates consistent patterns across compound components:

- **Wrapper Pattern**: Each compound component wraps a Radix UI primitive (e.g., `AccordionPrimitive.Root`, `TabsPrimitive.Root`)
- **Named Exports**: Multiple sub-components are exported as named exports for composition (e.g., `Accordion`, `AccordionItem`, `AccordionTrigger`, `AccordionContent`)
- **Data Attributes**: Each component sets `data-slot` attributes for styling targeting (e.g., `data-slot="accordion"`)
- **Utility Functions**: Uses `cn()` for class merging (combines `clsx` and `tailwind-merge`)
- **Variants**: Uses `cva()` from `class-variance-authority` for variant definitions

---

### **2. In-Depth Analysis of Key Compound Components**

#### **A. ACCORDION**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Context Usage**: Uses Radix UI's built-in context (AccordionPrimitive handles state internally)

**Sub-components**:
- `Accordion` (Root wrapper)
- `AccordionItem` (Individual accordion item)
- `AccordionTrigger` (Clickable header)
- `AccordionContent` (Expandable content)

**Key Features**:
- Wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Root`, `AccordionPrimitive.Item`, `AccordionPrimitive.Trigger`, `AccordionPrimitive.Content`
- Uses `data-slot` attributes for CSS targeting
- Implements animated icons (ChevronDown/ChevronUp) using `IconPlaceholder`
- Uses group selectors: `group-aria-expanded/accordion-trigger` to show/hide icons based on state

#### **B. TABS**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Context Usage**: Uses Radix UI's built-in context (TabsPrimitive handles state)

**Sub-components**:
- `Tabs` (Root with orientation support)
- `TabsList` (Container for triggers)
- `TabsTrigger` (Individual tab button)
- `TabsContent` (Content for each tab)

**Key Features**:
- **Orientation Handling**: 
  - Accepts `orientation` prop ("horizontal" or "vertical")
  - Uses `data-orientation` attribute
  - Applies conditional styling: `group-data-vertical/tabs:` and `group-data-horizontal/tabs:` for responsive layout
- **CVA Variants**: `tabsListVariants` with "default" and "line" variants
- **Complex Styling**: Uses pseudo-elements (`after:`) for underline indicators
- **Orientation-aware positioning**: Horizontal underline vs vertical border

#### **C. CAROUSEL** ⭐ Custom Context Example
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/carousel.tsx`

**Context Usage**: Creates its own React Context (best example in the library)

```typescript
const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

function useCarousel() {
  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
  }
  return context
}
```

**TypeScript Interfaces**:
```typescript
type CarouselProps = {
  opts?: CarouselOptions
  plugins?: CarouselPlugin
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  setApi?: (api: CarouselApi) => void
}

type CarouselContextProps = {
  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
  scrollPrev: () => void
  scrollNext: () => void
  canScrollPrev: boolean
  canScrollNext: boolean
} & CarouselProps
```

**Sub-components**:
- `Carousel` (Root provider with context)
- `CarouselContent` (Wrapper for items)
- `CarouselItem` (Individual slide)
- `CarouselPrevious` (Previous button)
- `CarouselNext` (Next button)
- `useCarousel` hook (Custom hook to access context)

**Key Features**:
- **Custom Context Management**: Wraps entire component tree with `CarouselContext.Provider`
- **Embla Carousel Integration**: Uses `useEmblaCarousel` hook from embla-carousel-react
- **Orientation Support**: Handles "horizontal" vs "vertical" scrolling
- **State Management**: Tracks `canScrollPrev` and `canScrollNext` states
- **Callback Handlers**: `scrollPrev()` and `scrollNext()` methods in context
- **Keyboard Navigation**: `handleKeyDown` for arrow key navigation
- **Consumer Pattern**: Sub-components use `useCarousel()` hook to access shared state

#### **D. NAVIGATION MENU**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/navigation-menu.tsx`

**Sub-components**:
- `NavigationMenu` (Root with optional viewport)
- `NavigationMenuList` (Container for items)
- `NavigationMenuItem` (Individual menu item)
- `NavigationMenuTrigger` (Trigger with chevron)
- `NavigationMenuContent` (Content area)
- `NavigationMenuViewport` (Animated viewport)
- `NavigationMenuLink` (Link element)
- `NavigationMenuIndicator` (Visual indicator)

**Key Features**:
- Uses CVA for `navigationMenuTriggerStyle` variants
- Conditional viewport rendering based on `viewport` prop
- Complex data attributes: `group-data-[viewport=false]/navigation-menu:` for styling variations

#### **E. DROPDOWN MENU**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`

**Sub-components** (15 total):
- `DropdownMenu` (Root)
- `DropdownMenuTrigger`
- `DropdownMenuContent` (Portal + content)
- `DropdownMenuItem` / `DropdownMenuCheckboxItem` / `DropdownMenuRadioItem`
- `DropdownMenuGroup`, `DropdownMenuLabel`, `DropdownMenuSeparator`
- `DropdownMenuSub`, `DropdownMenuSubTrigger`, `DropdownMenuSubContent`

**Key Features**:
- Nested menu support (Sub menus)
- Custom `inset` and `variant` props for styling
- Automatic Icon indicators via `ItemIndicator`

#### **F. MENUBAR**
**File**: `/apps/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474101635-1tujer/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/menubar.tsx`

**Similar structure to dropdown-menu** but with 13+ sub-components for app-like menu bars

---

### **3. TypeScript Patterns**

All components follow consistent TypeScript patterns:

```typescript
// Type extraction from Radix primitive
function Accordion({
  className,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Root>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="accordion"
      className={cn("default-styles", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}

// With custom props
function TabsList({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.List> &
  VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.List
      data-slot="tabs-list"
      data-variant={variant}
      className={cn(tabsListVariants({ variant }), className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

---

### **4. Key CSS/Styling Utilities**

**cn() Function** (from `lib/utils.ts`):
```typescript
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

**CVA Variants**:
```typescript
const tabsListVariants = cva(
  "base-classes",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "default-variant-classes",
        line: "line-variant-classes",
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
    },
  }
)
```

---

### **5. Orientation & Responsive Handling**

Components like **Tabs** and **Carousel** handle orientation with:

1. **Prop-based**: `orientation = "horizontal" | "vertical"`
2. **Data attributes**: `data-orientation={orientation}`
3. **Tailwind group selectors**:
   - `group-data-horizontal/tabs:` for horizontal styles
   - `group-data-vertical/tabs:` for vertical styles
4. **Conditional rendering**: Different layouts per orientation

Example from Carousel:
```typescript
orientation === "horizontal" 
  ? "-ml-4"              // Horizontal margin
  : "-mt-4 flex-col"     // Vertical margin with flex column
```

---

### **6. Important Patterns**

- **"use client" Directive**: Used at top of interactive components
- **Radix UI Dependencies**: All use radix-ui primitives as foundation
- **Portal Pattern**: Dropdown, popup, dialog components use Portals for proper z-stacking
- **Data Attributes**: Every sub-component has `data-slot` for CSS targeting and debugging
- **Icon Placeholder**: Custom component that allows multiple icon library sources
- **Group Selectors**: Uses Tailwind's `group/` and `group-data-*` for state-based child styling
- **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes (aria-expanded, role, aria-hidden, etc.)

---

### **7. No Stepper Component Found**

The shadcn/ui library does **NOT** currently include a Stepper/Steps component. The most similar component would be the **Tabs** component, which could serve as a base for building a custom Stepper.

---

### **File Locations**
- **Components**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/`
- **Utils**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils.ts`
- **Key components analyzed**:
  - accordion.tsx
  - tabs.tsx
  - carousel.tsx
  - navigation-menu.tsx
  - dropdown-menu.tsx
  - menubar.tsx
  - dialog.tsx
  - alert-dialog.tsx
  - popover.tsx
  - collapsible.tsx
  - breadcrumb.tsx

Focus on components in the `components/ui/` directory or similar. Read the actual file contents.

#### **A. ACCORDION**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Context Usage**: Uses Radix UI's built-in context (AccordionPrimitive handles state internally)

**Sub-components**:
- `Accordion` (Root wrapper)
- `AccordionItem` (Individual accordion item)
- `AccordionTrigger` (Clickable header)
- `AccordionContent` (Expandable content)

**Key Features**:
- Wraps `AccordionPrimitive.Root`, `AccordionPrimitive.Item`, `AccordionPrimitive.Trigger`, `AccordionPrimitive.Content`
- Uses `data-slot` attributes for CSS targeting
- Implements animated icons (ChevronDown/ChevronUp) using `IconPlaceholder`
- Uses group selectors: `group-aria-expanded/accordion-trigger` to show/hide icons based on state

#### **B. TABS**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Context Usage**: Uses Radix UI's built-in context (TabsPrimitive handles state)

**Sub-components**:
- `Tabs` (Root with orientation support)
- `TabsList` (Container for triggers)
- `TabsTrigger` (Individual tab button)
- `TabsContent` (Content for each tab)

**Key Features**:
- **Orientation Handling**: 
  - Accepts `orientation` prop ("horizontal" or "vertical")
  - Uses `data-orientation` attribute
  - Applies conditional styling: `group-data-vertical/tabs:` and `group-data-horizontal/tabs:` for responsive layout
- **CVA Variants**: `tabsListVariants` with "default" and "line" variants
- **Complex Styling**: Uses pseudo-elements (`after:`) for underline indicators
- **Orientation-aware positioning**: Horizontal underline vs vertical border

#### **C. CAROUSEL** ⭐ Custom Context Example
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/carousel.tsx`

**Context Usage**: Creates its own React Context (best example in the library)

```typescript
const CarouselContext = React.createContext<CarouselContextProps | null>(null)

function useCarousel() {
  const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext)
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a <Carousel />")
  }
  return context
}
```

**TypeScript Interfaces**:
```typescript
type CarouselProps = {
  opts?: CarouselOptions
  plugins?: CarouselPlugin
  orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  setApi?: (api: CarouselApi) => void
}

type CarouselContextProps = {
  carouselRef: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[0]
  api: ReturnType<typeof useEmblaCarousel>[1]
  scrollPrev: () => void
  scrollNext: () => void
  canScrollPrev: boolean
  canScrollNext: boolean
} & CarouselProps
```

**Sub-components**:
- `Carousel` (Root provider with context)
- `CarouselContent` (Wrapper for items)
- `CarouselItem` (Individual slide)
- `CarouselPrevious` (Previous button)
- `CarouselNext` (Next button)
- `useCarousel` hook (Custom hook to access context)

**Key Features**:
- **Custom Context Management**: Wraps entire component tree with `CarouselContext.Provider`
- **Embla Carousel Integration**: Uses `useEmblaCarousel` hook from embla-carousel-react
- **Orientation Support**: Handles "horizontal" vs "vertical" scrolling
- **State Management**: Tracks `canScrollPrev` and `canScrollNext` states
- **Callback Handlers**: `scrollPrev()` and `scrollNext()` methods in context
- **Keyboard Navigation**: `handleKeyDown` for arrow key navigation
- **Consumer Pattern**: Sub-components use `useCarousel()` hook to access shared state

#### **D. NAVIGATION MENU**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/navigation-menu.tsx`

**Sub-components**:
- `NavigationMenu` (Root with optional viewport)
- `NavigationMenuList` (Container for items)
- `NavigationMenuItem` (Individual menu item)
- `NavigationMenuTrigger` (Trigger with chevron)
- `NavigationMenuContent` (Content area)
- `NavigationMenuViewport` (Animated viewport)
- `NavigationMenuLink` (Link element)
- `NavigationMenuIndicator` (Visual indicator)

**Key Features**:
- Uses CVA for `navigationMenuTriggerStyle` variants
- Conditional viewport rendering based on `viewport` prop
- Complex data attributes: `group-data-[viewport=false]/navigation-menu:` for styling variations

#### **E. DROPDOWN MENU**
**File**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx`

**Sub-components** (15 total):
- `DropdownMenu` (Root)
- `DropdownMenuTrigger`
- `DropdownMenuContent` (Portal + content)
- `DropdownMenuItem` / `DropdownMenuCheckboxItem` / `DropdownMenuRadioItem`
- `DropdownMenuGroup`, `DropdownMenuLabel`, `DropdownMenuSeparator`
- `DropdownMenuSub`, `DropdownMenuSubTrigger`, `DropdownMenuSubContent`

**Key Features**:
- Nested menu support (Sub menus)
- Custom `inset` and `variant` props for styling
- Automatic Icon indicators via `ItemIndicator`

#### **F. MENUBAR**
**File**: `/apps/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474101635-1tujer/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/menubar.tsx`

**Similar structure to dropdown-menu** but with 13+ sub-components for app-like menu bars

---

### **3. TypeScript Patterns**

All components follow consistent TypeScript patterns:

```typescript
// Type extraction from Radix primitive
function Accordion({
  className,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof AccordionPrimitive.Root>) {
  return (
    <AccordionPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="accordion"
      className={cn("default-styles", className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}

// With custom props
function TabsList({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof TabsPrimitive.List> &
  VariantProps<typeof tabsListVariants>) {
  return (
    <TabsPrimitive.List
      data-slot="tabs-list"
      data-variant={variant}
      className={cn(tabsListVariants({ variant }), className)}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}
```

---

### **4. Key CSS/Styling Utilities**

**cn() Function** (from `lib/utils.ts`):
```typescript
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

**CVA Variants**:
```typescript
const tabsListVariants = cva(
  "base-classes",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "default-variant-classes",
        line: "line-variant-classes",
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
    },
  }
)
```

---

### **5. Orientation & Responsive Handling**

Components like **Tabs** and **Carousel** handle orientation with:

1. **Prop-based**: `orientation = "horizontal" | "vertical"`
2. **Data attributes**: `data-orientation={orientation}`
3. **Tailwind group selectors**:
   - `group-data-horizontal/tabs:` for horizontal styles
   - `group-data-vertical/tabs:` for vertical styles
4. **Conditional rendering**: Different layouts per orientation

Example from Carousel:
```typescript
orientation === "horizontal" 
  ? "-ml-4"              // Horizontal margin
  : "-mt-4 flex-col"     // Vertical margin with flex column
```

---

### **6. Important Patterns**

- **"use client" Directive**: Used at top of interactive components
- **Radix UI Dependencies**: All use radix-ui primitives as foundation
- **Portal Pattern**: Dropdown, popup, dialog components use Portals for proper z-stacking
- **Data Attributes**: Every sub-component has `data-slot` for CSS targeting and debugging
- **Icon Placeholder**: Custom component that allows multiple icon library sources
- **Group Selectors**: Uses Tailwind's `group/` and `group-data-*` for state-based child styling
- **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes (aria-expanded, role, aria-hidden, etc.)

---

### **7. No Stepper Component Found**

The shadcn/ui library does **NOT** currently include a Stepper/Steps component. The most similar component would be the **Tabs** component, which could serve as a base for building a custom Stepper.

---

### **File Locations**
- **Components**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/`
- **Utils**: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils.ts`
- **Key components analyzed**:
  - accordion.tsx
  - tabs.tsx
  - carousel.tsx
  - navigation-menu.tsx
  - dropdown-menu.tsx
  - menubar.tsx
  - dialog.tsx
  - alert-dialog.tsx
  - popover.tsx
  - collapsible.tsx
  - breadcrumb.tsx

Here are 3 examples: **Accordion Example** (`accordion-demo.tsx`): ```tsx import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( Product Information ... {/* more items */} ) } ``` **Tabs Example** (`tabs-demo.tsx`): ```tsx import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card" import { Input } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input" import { Label } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/label" import { Tabs, TabsContent, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs" export default function TabsDemo() { return (
Account Password {/* content */} {/* more tabs */}
) } ``` **Dialog Example** (`dialog-demo.tsx`): ```tsx import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" import { Dialog, DialogClose, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/dialog" import { Input } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input" import { Label } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/label" export default function DialogDemo() { return (
Edit profile ... {/* form content */}
) } ``` ### 3. **Import Pattern & Export Style** - **Default Export Only**: All demo files use `export default function ComponentNameDemo()` - **Import Source**: Components are imported from `@/registry/[style-name]/ui/[component-name]` - Full path: `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion` - **Icon Imports**: External icons from `lucide-react` (e.g., `CheckCircle2Icon`, `CodeIcon`) - **No State Complexity**: Examples are purely presentational (no hooks, state, or complex logic) ### 4. **Registry Configuration** Registry metadata lives in `_registry.ts` files within each style directory. Structure: ```typescript { name: "accordion-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["accordion"], // Component dependencies files: [ { path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], } ``` Path: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` ### 5. **How Demos Are Rendered in Documentation** **In MDX Files** (`content/docs/components/radix/accordion.mdx`): ```mdx ``` **The Rendering Pipeline**: 1. MDX calls `` 2. `ComponentPreview` component (`/apps/v4/components/component-preview.tsx`) loads the demo using `getRegistryComponent()` 3. `getRegistryComponent()` looks up demo in `ExamplesIndex` (autogenerated index) 4. Component is wrapped in `ComponentPreviewTabs` with preview + source code tabs 5. Demo renders as a React component inside a preview container Path: `/apps/v4/app/(app)/docs/[[...slug]]/page.tsx` (renders MDX) ### 6. **Autogenerated Examples Index** Path: `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` Auto-generated by build script. Contains: ```typescript export const ExamplesIndex: Record> = { radix: { "accordion-demo": { name: "accordion-demo", filePath: "examples/radix/accordion-demo.tsx", component: React.lazy(async () => { const mod = await import("./radix/accordion-demo") // Dynamic export detection return { default: mod.default || mod[exportName] } }), }, // ... more examples } } ``` ### 7. **Key Wrapper Patterns** - **Container wrapper**: Simple `
` for layout - **Form wrapper**: Dialog examples wrap content in `
` for semantic HTML - **Dialog pattern**: Uses composition with `DialogTrigger`, `DialogContent`, `DialogHeader`, etc. - **Card pattern**: Tabs demo uses `Card` component as content container - **No Provider wrappers**: Examples assume providers are already in the app ### 8. **File Naming Conventions** Pattern: `[base-component]-[variant-or-type].tsx` - Base: `accordion`, `tabs`, `dialog`, `alert`, `button` - Variant: `demo`, `destructive`, `outline`, `secondary`, `responsive`, `borders`, `multiple`, etc. - Examples: - `accordion-demo.tsx` (basic demo) - `alert-destructive.tsx` (variant demo) - `dialog-close-button.tsx` (feature variant) - `tabs-demo.tsx` (main demo) ### 9. **Import Path Transformation** The registry system transforms import paths when generating docs/installation code: - From: `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion` - To: `@/components/ui/accordion` (for users) This happens in `/apps/v4/lib/registry.ts` via `fixImport()` function. ### Key Files Reference: - Demo files: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` - Registry metadata: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Rendering component: `/apps/v4/components/component-preview.tsx` - Registry loader: `/apps/v4/lib/registry.ts` - Examples index: `/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx` (autogenerated) - MDX integration: `/apps/v4/mdx-components.tsx` - Docs pages: `/apps/v4/content/docs/components/[base]/[component].mdx`

Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: shadcn/ui Component Library Structure

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui project structure. Here's everything you need to understand how to add a new "Stepper" component:

---

### 1. ROOT DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474101635-1tujer/`

Key directories:
- **`apps/v4`** - Main v4 application (Next.js based, the primary component library)
- **`packages/shadcn`** - Core CLI/library package containing schema definitions
- **`registry/`** - Component registry directory (auto-generated and manual)
- **`styles/`** - Output directory with styled component variants
- **`examples/`** - Example implementations for each component
- **`templates/`** - Project templates
- **`scripts/`** - Build scripts including `build-registry.mts`
- **`content/docs/`** - Documentation files

---

### 2. REGISTRY STRUCTURE & SCHEMA

**Registry Schema Location:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

**Key Registry Item Types:**
```typescript
"registry:lib" | "registry:block" | "registry:component" | "registry:ui" | 
"registry:hook" | "registry:page" | "registry:file" | "registry:theme" | 
"registry:style" | "registry:item" | "registry:base" | "registry:font"
```

**Registry Item Structure (Zod Schema):**
```typescript
{
  $schema?: string                    // Optional JSON schema reference
  name: string                        // REQUIRED: kebab-case identifier
  type: RegistryItemType              // REQUIRED: one of the types above
  title?: string                      // Display title
  author?: string                     // Author name (min 2 chars)
  description?: string                // Component description
  extends?: string                    // For inheritance
  dependencies?: string[]             // npm packages (e.g., "radix-ui")
  devDependencies?: string[]          // Dev packages
  registryDependencies?: string[]     // Other registry components (e.g., ["button"])
  files: RegistryItemFile[]          // REQUIRED: file paths/content
  tailwind?: {                        // Optional Tailwind config
    config?: {
      content?: string[]
      theme?: Record<string, any>
      plugins?: string[]
    }
  }
  cssVars?: {                         // CSS variables
    theme?: Record<string, string>
    light?: Record<string, string>
    dark?: Record<string, string>
  }
  css?: Record<string, any>          // Raw CSS
  envVars?: Record<string, string>    // Environment variables
  meta?: Record<string, any>          // Custom metadata
  docs?: string                       // Documentation link
  categories?: string[]               // Component categories
}
```

**File Schema:**
```typescript
{
  path: string                        // File path (relative)
  type: RegistryItemType              // File type
  content?: string                    // Optional inline content
  target?: string                     // REQUIRED for registry:file, registry:page
}
```

---

### 3. HOW COMPONENTS ARE REGISTERED

**Main Registry File:** `apps/v4/registry.json` (auto-generated, ~150KB)

**Example Registry Entry (from registry.json):**
```json
{
  "name": "accordion",
  "type": "registry:ui",
  "dependencies": ["@radix-ui/react-accordion"],
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx",
      "type": "registry:ui"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Complex Component with Dependencies (sidebar):**
```json
{
  "name": "sidebar",
  "type": "registry:ui",
  "dependencies": ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  "registryDependencies": ["button", "separator", "sheet", "tooltip", "input", "use-mobile", "skeleton"],
  "files": [...],
  "tailwind": { /* theme extensions */ },
  "cssVars": { /* CSS variables */ }
}
```

**Source Registry Files:** `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`
- Manually maintained TypeScript file
- Exports `ui: Registry["items"]` array
- Gets compiled into registry.json during build

---

### 4. COMPONENT FILE STRUCTURE & LOCATIONS

**Directory Layout for a Style Combination:**

```
apps/v4/
├── registry/
│   ├── new-york-v4/           # Generated style variant
│   │   ├── ui/                # Component implementations
│   │   │   ├── accordion.tsx
│   │   │   ├── button.tsx
│   │   │   └── _registry.ts   # Manual registry definitions
│   │   ├── hooks/             # Custom hooks
│   │   ├── lib/               # Utilities (e.g., cn(), utils)
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── bases/                 # Base style definitions
│   │   ├── base/              # Base UI components
│   │   └── radix/             # Radix UI components
│   └── config.ts              # Configuration exports
│
├── styles/                    # Final compiled output per style
│   ├── radix-nova/
│   │   └── ui/                # Compiled components
│   ├── radix-lyra/
│   ├── base-vega/
│   └── ...
│
├── examples/                  # Demo implementations
│   ├── radix/                 # Base examples
│   │   ├── accordion-demo.tsx
│   │   ├── accordion-basic.tsx
│   │   ├── accordion-card.tsx
│   │   └── ...
│   ├── base/                  # Other base examples
│   └── __index__.tsx          # Auto-generated index
```

---

### 5. REGISTRY FILE FORMATS

**Registry Schema Location:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`
**Public JSON Schema:** `apps/v4/public/schema.json`

**Two Registry File Formats:**

1. **registry.json** - Full registry with all items:
   - Auto-generated by `build-registry.mts`
   - Contains all component metadata
   - ~150KB file with complete item definitions

2. **directory.json** - Lightweight directory structure:
   - Auto-generated by `build-registry.mts`
   - Faster for lookups
   - ~450KB file

---

### 6. BUILD PIPELINE

**Build Script:** `apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts`

**Execution Order:**
1. Build `registry/bases/__index__.tsx` from authored base registries
2. Build temporary styled registries under `registry/<base-style>`
3. Build main `registry/__index__.tsx` for runtime lookup
4. Build `examples/__index__.tsx` from demo files

6. Copy compiled `ui/*` from temporary registries into `styles/<style>/ui`
7. Build `styles/<style>/ui-rtl` for base-nova and radix-nova only
8. Format generated outputs
9. Clean up temporary files

**Key Constants:**
- `STYLE_COMBINATIONS = BASES × STYLES` (currently 2 bases × multiple styles)
- Base names: "radix", "base"
- Style names: "nova", "vega", "maia", "lyra", "mira", "luma"

---

### 7. CONFIGURATION & TOOLING

**package.json (root):**
- **Package Manager:** pnpm@9.0.6
- **Monorepo:** pnpm workspaces
- **Build Tool:** Turbo

**components.json** (app config):
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema.json",
  "style": "new-york",
  "rsc": true,
  "tsx": true,
  "tailwind": {
    "config": "",
    "css": "app/globals.css",
    "baseColor": "neutral",
    "cssVariables": true
  },
  "aliases": {
    "components": "@/components",
    "utils": "@/lib/utils",
    "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui",
    "lib": "@/lib",
    "hooks": "@/hooks"
  },

}
```

**next.config.mjs:**
- Turbopack enabled for fast builds
- File tracing includes: `./registry/**/*`, `./styles/**/*`
- Component redirects for default base

---

### 8. EXAMPLE COMPONENT PATTERNS

**Simple Component (Button):**
```typescript
// File: registry/new-york-v4/ui/button.tsx
import * as React from "react"
import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const buttonVariants = cva(
  "inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center...",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: { /* CVA variants */ },
      size: { /* size variants */ }
    },
    defaultVariants: { variant: "default", size: "default" }
  }
)

function Button({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  asChild = false,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> & VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants>) {
  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
  return (
    <Comp
      data-slot="button"
      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}

export { Button, buttonVariants }
```

**Accordion (With Dependencies & Radix):**
```typescript
// Uses @radix-ui/react-accordion dependency

// Subcomponents: Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent
```

**Sidebar (Complex with Context & Multiple Dependencies):**
- Uses `useIsMobile` hook
- Manages internal state with context
- Dependent on: button, separator, sheet, tooltip, input, skeleton
- Includes tailwind config extensions for sidebar colors
- Defines CSS variables for theming

### 9. EXAMPLE DEMOS STRUCTURE

**Example Files Location:** `apps/v4/examples/radix/`

**Pattern:**

```typescript
import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger } 
  from "@/styles/radix-nova/ui/accordion"

export default function AccordionDemo() {
  return (
    <Accordion type="single" collapsible defaultValue="shipping">
      <AccordionItem value="shipping">
        <AccordionTrigger>Question?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>Answer text</AccordionContent>
      </AccordionItem>
    </Accordion>

}
```

### 11. REGISTRY ENTRY FORMAT FOR STEPPER COMPONENT

To add a Stepper component, you would need a registry entry like:

```typescript
{
  name: "stepper",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],  // if using Radix primitives
  registryDependencies: ["button"],  // depends on button component
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
  // Optional: tailwind config extensions
  tailwind: {
    config: {
      theme: {
        extend: {
          // Stepper-specific styles
        }
      }
    }
  },
  // Optional: CSS variables for theming
  cssVars: {
    light: {
      "stepper-bg": "0 0% 100%",
      "stepper-fg": "0 0% 0%"
    },
    dark: {
      "stepper-bg": "0 0% 10%",
      "stepper-fg": "0 0% 100%"
    }
  }
}
```

---

### 12. SUMMARY: FILES YOU'LL NEED TO CREATE/MODIFY

For a complete Stepper implementation:

1. **Component Implementation:**
   - `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` - Main component
   - `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` - Base implementation (if using Radix)
   - `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` - Alternative base if needed

2. **Registry Entry:**
   - Add entry to `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`

3. **Examples:**

5. **Run Build:**

All registry files are **auto-generated** - you only edit the `_registry.ts` and component files directly.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: shadcn/ui Component Library Structure

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui project structure. Here's everything you need to understand how to add a new "Stepper" component:

---

### 1. ROOT DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474101635-1tujer/`

Key directories:
- **`apps/v4`** - Main v4 application (Next.js based, the primary component library)
- **`packages/shadcn`** - Core CLI/library package containing schema definitions
- **`registry/`** - Component registry directory (auto-generated and manual)
- **`styles/`** - Output directory with styled component variants
- **`examples/`** - Example implementations for each component
- **`templates/`** - Project templates
- **`scripts/`** - Build scripts including `build-registry.mts`
- **`content/docs/`** - Documentation files

---

### 2. REGISTRY STRUCTURE & SCHEMA

**Registry Schema Location:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

**Key Registry Item Types:**
```typescript
"registry:lib" | "registry:block" | "registry:component" | "registry:ui" | 
"registry:hook" | "registry:page" | "registry:file" | "registry:theme" | 
"registry:style" | "registry:item" | "registry:base" | "registry:font"
```

**Registry Item Structure (Zod Schema):**
```typescript
{
  $schema?: string                    // Optional JSON schema reference
  name: string                        // REQUIRED: kebab-case identifier
  type: RegistryItemType              // REQUIRED: one of the types above
  title?: string                      // Display title
  author?: string                     // Author name (min 2 chars)
  description?: string                // Component description
  extends?: string                    // For inheritance
  dependencies?: string[]             // npm packages (e.g., "radix-ui")
  devDependencies?: string[]          // Dev packages
  registryDependencies?: string[]     // Other registry components (e.g., ["button"])
  files: RegistryItemFile[]          // REQUIRED: file paths/content
  tailwind?: {                        // Optional Tailwind config
    config?: {
      content?: string[]
      theme?: Record<string, any>
      plugins?: string[]
    }
  }
  cssVars?: {                         // CSS variables
    theme?: Record<string, string>
    light?: Record<string, string>
    dark?: Record<string, string>
  }
  css?: Record<string, any>          // Raw CSS
  envVars?: Record<string, string>    // Environment variables
  meta?: Record<string, any>          // Custom metadata
  docs?: string                       // Documentation link
  categories?: string[]               // Component categories
}
```

**File Schema:**
```typescript
{
  path: string                        // File path (relative)
  type: RegistryItemType              // File type
  content?: string                    // Optional inline content
  target?: string                     // REQUIRED for registry:file, registry:page
}
```

---

### 3. HOW COMPONENTS ARE REGISTERED

**Main Registry File:** `apps/v4/registry.json` (auto-generated, ~150KB)

**Example Registry Entry (from registry.json):**
```json
{
  "name": "accordion",
  "type": "registry:ui",
  "dependencies": ["@radix-ui/react-accordion"],
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx",
      "type": "registry:ui"
    }
  ]
}
```

**Complex Component with Dependencies (sidebar):**
```json
{
  "name": "sidebar",
  "type": "registry:ui",
  "dependencies": ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  "registryDependencies": ["button", "separator", "sheet", "tooltip", "input", "use-mobile", "skeleton"],
  "files": [...],
  "tailwind": { /* theme extensions */ },
  "cssVars": { /* CSS variables */ }
}
```

**Source Registry Files:** `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`
- Manually maintained TypeScript file
- Exports `ui: Registry["items"]` array
- Gets compiled into registry.json during build

### 4. COMPONENT FILE STRUCTURE & LOCATIONS

```
apps/v4/
├── registry/
│   ├── new-york-v4/           # Generated style variant
│   │   ├── ui/                # Component implementations
│   │   │   ├── accordion.tsx
│   │   │   ├── button.tsx
```

content.thinking:
Now I understand the architecture. The key insight is:

1. `bases/radix/ui/` - Contains the "base" component implementation (abstract, with `cn-` prefixed classes for theming, using `IconPlaceholder` for icon flexibility)
2. `new-york-v4/ui/` - Contains the "new-york-v4 style" implementation (concrete, with actual Tailwind classes, using lucide-react icons directly)
3. `bases/radix/examples/` - Contains example files for the base/radix style


4. `new-york-v4/examples/` - Contains example files for the new-york-v4 style

The `new-york-v4` version applies specific visual styling while the `bases/radix` version stays abstract and themeable. To add a new component, I need to create the base implementation and registry entry, then create the styled new-york-v4 version with its own registry entry.

I also need to add a demo example and register it, then check what the `bases/base/ui/` directory looks like since I'll need to create a stepper component there too.

Here's my understanding of the full structure:

**Two "base" implementations:**
1. `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/` - Uses Radix UI primitives, has `cn-*` class names, uses `IconPlaceholder`, imports from `@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils`
2. `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/` - Uses @base-ui/react primitives (same pattern, different imports)


3. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` - Uses specific Tailwind classes and lucide-react icons directly, imports from `@/lib/utils`

Then I need to set up the example files and registry entries: comprehensive examples for the Radix base with multiple sub-examples, a simple demo for the styled version, and four registry files to register each stepper component.

Actually, I'm realizing the stepper is a custom context component without any Radix primitive backing, so there won't be any radix-ui dependencies to declare. Let me check how other non-Radix components handle their registry entries. Since there's no Stepper primitive in either Radix or base-ui, both `bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` and `bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` will use the same custom context pattern with plain HTML. Let me check how carousel differs between the two bases to see if there are any other distinctions I'm missing.

Looking at the carousel implementations, the only real difference is the import path for utilities — `bases/base` imports from its own utils directory while `bases/radix` imports from its utils. So I'll follow that same pattern for the stepper files, then create the styled version for new-york-v4 and the example files.

Now I'm realizing the user said to only create new files and add registry entries, but adding registry entries means I have to modify the `_registry.ts` files. Let me clarify what that actually means in the context of their request.

I think when they said "existing component files," they meant the actual UI component implementations like accordion.tsx or tabs.tsx—not the registry configuration files. So modifying the registry files to add entries should be acceptable.

For the stepper component itself, I'm planning a structure with a root Stepper container that manages state through context, then individual StepperItem wrappers for each step, along with sub-components for the trigger, title, description, separator, and content areas.

Setting up the context to track the active step value and orientation, plus defining the API so users can control which step is active and respond to changes...

Building out the component structure with trigger elements that contain the indicator, title, and description, along with separators between steps and content panels for each step...

Now I'm thinking through the API design more carefully. The user needs a stepper that supports both controlled and uncontrolled patterns, similar to how other components in this codebase work. I'm considering whether to let users manage the active step externally or handle it internally with a default value, or ideally support both approaches.

For the component structure, I'm planning a root Stepper component that wraps individual StepperItems, each containing a trigger with an indicator, title, and description, plus a separator line connecting to the next step. The content panels would sit separately below the step navigation. Building the stepper component... Building the stepper component...

I need to set up a context system where the root `Stepper` component tracks the active step and passes that down through `StepperItem` context so child components like `StepperContent` and `StepperTrigger` know their state. The abstract base will use themeable class prefixes and utility imports following the existing pattern.

Now I'm thinking through the visual design for the styled version—step indicators as circles with numbers that change to checkmarks when completed, with connecting lines between them that orient horizontally or vertically depending on the layout, and I need to handle three distinct states: active, completed, and pending steps.

To make this work, the Stepper component needs to track which step is current and which are already done, so I'll use data attributes to mark each step's state and determine that state either through explicit props or by computing it based on step ordering and the current value. state from the `value` prop relative to each `StepperItem`'s value, but this requires knowing the order. 

Actually, looking at how other libraries handle this, the most common approach for shadcn-style is:
1. Each `StepperItem` accepts a `value` and optionally `completed` prop
2. The state is determined by: if the item's value === stepper's value → "active", if completed prop is set → "completed", otherwise → "pending"
3. User controls what's "completed" externally

OR:

The Stepper could accept a `steps` prop (ordered array) and compute "completed" based on position relative to current step.

For simplicity and flexibility, I'll go with the approach where the Stepper has `value` and ordered step list knowledge, with `StepperItem` computing its state based on context. To know the order, I can either have the Stepper accept `steps` as a prop or use a simpler approach where I pass `value` to the root and let `StepperItem` handle its own `value` and optional `completed` prop. The cleanest way that requires no extra props is having the user explicitly mark completed steps or auto-compute based on step order. I'm settling on a context-based approach that mirrors the existing Carousel pattern—tracking the active step value and a change handler, with explicit `completed` and `disabled` props on each StepperItem to control state rather than auto-computing from step order.

Now I'm checking the registry and import patterns to see how other non-Radix components are structured in this codebase.

Structure: ```typescript "use client" // Context definition const CarouselContext = React.createContext(null) function useCarousel() { const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a ") return context } // Root component wraps with Provider function Carousel({ orientation = "horizontal", className, children, ...props }) { return (

`bases/radix/ui/`: `import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils"` - `bases/base/ui/`: `import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils"` ## Existing Pattern: Registry entries `_registry.ts` (bases/radix/ui) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/stepper", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx", }, }, } ``` `_registry.ts` (new-york-v4/ui) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], } ``` `_registry.ts` (examples) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` `_registry.ts` (bases/radix/examples) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper-example", title: "Stepper", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "example"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-example.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` ## Stepper Component Requirements Sub-components needed: 1. `Stepper` — Root container, manages state (value/defaultValue/onValueChange, orientation) 2. `StepperItem` — Individual step wrapper (has `value`, `completed`, `disabled` props) 3. `StepperTrigger` — Clickable button to navigate to a step 4. `StepperIndicator` — Visual indicator (shows step number or check icon) 5. `StepperTitle` — Step title 6. `StepperDescription` — Step description 7. `StepperSeparator` — Connector line between steps 8. `StepperContent` — Content panel for a step (shown when step is active) Two contexts: - `StepperContext` — `{ value, onValueChange, orientation }` - `StepperItemContext` — `{ value, state: "active"|"completed"|"pending", disabled }` State computed: item.value === stepper.value → "active"; explicit `completed` prop → "completed"; else → "pending". Data attributes to use: `data-slot`, `data-orientation`, `data-state` (active/completed/pending), `data-disabled` Orientation handling: - Horizontal: steps laid out in a row (flex-row), separator is horizontal line - Vertical: steps stacked (flex-col), separator is vertical line ## What to Plan Design a complete step-by-step implementation plan covering: 1. Precise component code design for each file 4. Alphabetical placement of registry entries (since existing entries are alphabetical) 5. Verification steps (how to confirm it works) Please produce a detailed plan with the exact code for each file. 1	import * as React from "react"
2	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
3	import { Slot } from "radix-ui"
4	
5	import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils"
6	
7	const buttonVariants = cva(
8	  "cn-button group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none select-none disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0",
9	  {
10	    variants: {
11	      variant: {
12	        default: "cn-button-variant-default",
13	        outline: "cn-button-variant-outline",
14	        secondary: "cn-button-variant-secondary",
15	        ghost: "cn-button-variant-ghost",
16	        destructive: "cn-button-variant-destructive",
17	        link: "cn-button-variant-link",
18	      },
19	      size: {
20	        default: "cn-button-size-default",
21	        xs: "cn-button-size-xs",
22	        sm: "cn-button-size-sm",
23	        lg: "cn-button-size-lg",
24	        icon: "cn-button-size-icon",
25	        "icon-xs": "cn-button-size-icon-xs",
26	        "icon-sm": "cn-button-size-icon-sm",
27	        "icon-lg": "cn-button-size-icon-lg",
28	      },
29	    },
30	    defaultVariants: {
31	      variant: "default",
32	      size: "default",
33	    },
34	  }
35	)
36	
37	function Button({
38	  className,
39	  variant = "default",
40	  size = "default",
41	  asChild = false,
42	  ...props
43	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
44	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
45	    asChild?: boolean
46	  }) {
47	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
48	
49	  return (
50	    <Comp
51	      data-slot="button"
52	      data-variant={variant}
53	      data-size={size}
54	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
55	      {...props}
56	    />
57	  )
58	}
59	
60	export { Button, buttonVariants }
61

Do NOT write any code — just produce a clear, step-by-step implementation plan. ## Project Architecture The project lives at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474101635-1tujer/apps/v4/`. There are **three layers** of component files: ### Layer 1: Base/Abstract components - `registry/bases/radix/ui/` — Uses Radix UI primitives where available, custom context for custom components. Uses `cn-*` prefixed classes for themeable styling, `IconPlaceholder` for icons, imports `cn` from `@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils` - `registry/bases/base/ui/` — Uses @base-ui/react primitives. Identical patterns to radix, just different import paths (`@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils`, different icon primitives) ### Layer 2: Styled components (new-york-v4) - `registry/new-york-v4/ui/` — Concrete visual styling with specific Tailwind classes, uses lucide-react icons, imports `cn` from `@/lib/utils` ### Layer 3: Demo examples - `registry/bases/radix/examples/` — Comprehensive examples with multiple sub-variants - `registry/new-york-v4/examples/` — Simple default export demo ### Registry files (add entries to these, do not overwrite) - `registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` - `registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` - `registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` - `registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - `registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` ## Existing Pattern: Carousel (Custom Context) The Carousel component is the best reference for custom context. Structure: ```typescript "use client" // Context definition const CarouselContext = React.createContext(null) function useCarousel() { const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a ") return context } // Root component wraps with Provider function Carousel({ orientation = "horizontal", className, children, ...props }) { return (
{children}
) } ``` ## Existing Pattern: Tabs (Orientation) Tabs handles horizontal/vertical via `data-orientation` and Tailwind group selectors: ```typescript className={cn("cn-tabs group/tabs flex data-horizontal:flex-col", className)} // Children use: group-data-vertical/tabs: and group-data-horizontal/tabs: ``` ## Existing Pattern: Base vs Radix difference The `bases/radix/ui/` and `bases/base/ui/` versions are nearly identical — only import paths differ: - `bases/radix/ui/`: `import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils"` - `bases/base/ui/`: `import { cn } from "@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils"` ## Existing Pattern: Registry entries `_registry.ts` (bases/radix/ui) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], meta: { links: { docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/stepper", examples: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx", }, }, } ``` `_registry.ts` (new-york-v4/ui) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], } ``` `_registry.ts` (examples) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` `_registry.ts` (bases/radix/examples) format: ```typescript { name: "stepper-example", title: "Stepper", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "example"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-example.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` ## Stepper Component Requirements Sub-components needed: 1. `Stepper` — Root container, manages state (value/defaultValue/onValueChange, orientation) 2. `StepperItem` — Individual step wrapper (has `value`, `completed`, `disabled` props) 3. `StepperTrigger` — Clickable button to navigate to a step 4. `StepperIndicator` — Visual indicator (shows step number or check icon) 5. `StepperTitle` — Step title 6. `StepperDescription` — Step description 7. `StepperSeparator` — Connector line between steps 8. `StepperContent` — Content panel for a step (shown when step is active) Two contexts: - `StepperContext` — `{ value, onValueChange, orientation }` - `StepperItemContext` — `{ value, state: "active"|"completed"|"pending", disabled }` State computed: item.value === stepper.value → "active"; explicit `completed` prop → "completed"; else → "pending". Data attributes to use: `data-slot`, `data-orientation`, `data-state` (active/completed/pending), `data-disabled` Orientation handling: - Horizontal: steps laid out in a row (flex-row), separator is horizontal line - Vertical: steps stacked (flex-col), separator is vertical line ## What to Plan Design a complete step-by-step implementation plan covering: 1. All files to CREATE (new files only, not existing components): - `registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` - `registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` - `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` - `registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` - `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` 2. All registry files to APPEND TO (adding new entries only): - `registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` - `registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` - `registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` - `registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - `registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` 3. Precise component code design for each file 4. Alphabetical placement of registry entries (since existing entries are alphabetical) 5. Verification steps (how to confirm it works) Please produce a detailed plan with the exact code for each file. Here's the complete implementation plan for the Stepper compound component:

## What will be created

**5 new component files:**
1. `registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` — abstract base (custom context, `cn-*` tokens, `IconPlaceholder`)
2. `registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` — identical except imports from `bases/base` path
3. `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` — styled with concrete Tailwind classes + `lucide-react`
4. `registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` — comprehensive multi-variant example
5. `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` — minimal default demo

**5 registry entries appended** (alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`) to:
- `bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` (line 831)
- `bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` (line 831)
- `new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (line 563)
- `bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` (line 765)
- `new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (line 2124)

## Architecture decisions
- Follows the **Carousel pattern**: two `React.createContext` values (`StepperContext` + `StepperItemContext`), no external primitive
- **`StepperSeparator`** is a sibling between `<StepperItem>` elements (like `BreadcrumbSeparator`), reads `orientation` from context
- **`StepperContent`** is a child inside `<StepperItem>`, renders `null` when its step isn't active
- Step state (`active` / `completed` / `pending`) is computed from `value` comparison + explicit `completed` prop
- Controlled + uncontrolled both supported via `value` / `defaultValue` / `onValueChange`
- `data-slot`, `data-state`, `data-orientation`, `data-disabled` attributes on all elements

## Sub-components
`Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent` + exported `useStepper` and `useStepperItem` hooks

| Radix example | `registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` — 5 sub-variants |
| ny-v4 demo | `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` — minimal controlled demo |

### What gets appended (5 registry entries, no existing files modified)
All `stepper` entries inserted **alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`** in each `_registry.ts`.

### Architecture decisions
- **Follows the Carousel pattern** — pure React, two custom contexts (`StepperContext` + `StepperItemContext`), no Radix/base-ui primitive
- **`StepperSeparator`** is a sibling *between* `<StepperItem>` elements (like `BreadcrumbSeparator`); reads `orientation` from context → `h-px flex-1 mt-4` (horizontal) or `w-px h-6 ml-4` (vertical)
- **`StepperContent`** is a child *inside* `<StepperItem>`; returns `null` when its step isn't active
- **Step state** (`active` / `completed` / `pending`) is computed from `value` comparison + explicit `completed` prop on `<StepperItem>`
- **Controlled + uncontrolled** both supported via `value` / `defaultValue` / `onValueChange`
- `data-slot`, `data-state`, `data-orientation`, `data-disabled` on every element

### Sub-components
`Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent` + exported `useStepper` / `useStepperItem` hooks

5	## Stepper Component — Implementation Plan
6	
7	### What gets created (5 new files)
8	
9	| Layer | File |
10	|-------|------|
11	| Base (radix) | `registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` — abstract, `cn-*` tokens, `IconPlaceholder` |
12	| Base (base-ui) | `registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` — identical, just different `lib/utils` import path |
13	| Styled | `registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` — concrete Tailwind classes + `lucide-react` |
14	| Radix example | `registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` — 5 sub-variants |
15	| ny-v4 demo | `registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` — minimal controlled demo |
16	
17	### What gets appended (5 registry entries, no existing files modified)
18	All `stepper` entries inserted **alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`** in each `_registry.ts`.
19	
20	### Architecture decisions
21	- **Follows the Carousel pattern** — pure React, two custom contexts (`StepperContext` + `StepperItemContext`), no Radix/base-ui primitive
22	- **`StepperSeparator`** is a sibling *between* `<StepperItem>` elements (like `BreadcrumbSeparator`); reads `orientation` from context → `h-px flex-1 mt-4` (horizontal) or `w-px h-6 ml-4` (vertical)
23	- **`StepperContent`** is a child *inside* `<StepperItem>`; returns `null` when its step isn't active
24	- **Step state** (`active` / `completed` / `pending`) is computed from `value` comparison + explicit `completed` prop on `<StepperItem>`
25	- **Controlled + uncontrolled** both supported via `value` / `defaultValue` / `onValueChange`
26	- `data-slot`, `data-state`, `data-orientation`, `data-disabled` on every element
27	
28	### Sub-components
29	`Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent` + exported `useStepper` / `useStepperItem` hooks
